sglang.title: Logprobs Refractor
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
What does this PR do?

 Refractor all logits and logprobs handling logic, including:

Clarify the naming rules: *_token_logprobs stands for each token' logprobs, *_prompt_logprob stands for the sum of logprobs of a segment of prompt.
Split the *_token_logprobs into prefill_token_logprobs and decode_

vllm.title: [Core] remove cupy dependency
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Separate code from #3442 , only remove cupy dependency.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] More faithful implementation of Gemma
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes #3411
This PR reflects the recent bug fix in HF Gemma model. Please refer to huggingface/transformers#29402 for more details.
NOTE: There's still one minor difference between vLLM's Gemma and HF's: vLLM's RMS norm computes output * (1 + weight) in BF16 while the correct implementation uses FP3

vllm.title: [Bugfix] [Hotfix] fix nccl library name
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Usually, the convention for dynamic shared library is: for nccl version 2.18.3, there would be a file libnccl.so.2.18.3, and the following files linked to that file:

libnccl.so.2.18
libnccl.so.2
libncclso.

This is often the case for system-installed nccl, e.g. our CI machine, in /usr/lib/x86_64-li

vllm.title: [Model] Add support for DBRX
vllm.author: megha95
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds supports for DBRX. DBRX is a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model trained by Databricks with 132B total parameters and 36B live parameters. More details about the model can be found at: DBRX Technical Blog
Model weights can be found in HF repo:

vllm.title: [Misc] add the "download-dir" option to the latency/throughput benchmarks
vllm.author: AmadeusChan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Previously, the latency and throughput benchmarks do not support the --download-dir option. This PR adds the option to the benchmarks scripts so that users can specify the model download directory.

vllm.title: feat(benchmarks): Add Prefix Caching Benchmark to Serving Benchmark
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Reopens #3194 that was closed due to fork cleanup
Additional changes not mentioned in #3194 :

Feature to save individual token latencies (itl) per request to the result json for further debugging
Default vllm benchmark to OpenAI Completion API
If streaming is supported, count output tokens by count

vllm.title: Add support for Cohere's Command-R model
vllm.author: zeppombal
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
https://huggingface.co/CohereForAI/c4ai-command-r-v01
The PR implements the C4AI Command-R model requested in #3330 and #3403.

vllm.title: [Model] Add support for xverse
vllm.author: hxer7963
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add support for xverse models.
We tested the xverse 7B/13B/65B chat models and the quantized GPTQ model locally, and vllm responses are normal.
You can verify by downloading xverse models from Hugging Face (https://huggingface.co/xverse) or Modelscope (https://www.modelscope.cn/search?search=xverse)

vllm.title: [CI/Build] update default number of jobs and nvcc threads to avoid overloading the system
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Previously, the default number of nvcc threads is 8, and the default number of jobs is half as the number of CPU cores (os.cpu_count() / (nvcc_threads / 4)), leading to 4 * os.cpu_count() threads compiling in parallel. This often crashes the system, as reported in #3601 .
This PR changes the default

vllm.title: [Kernel] Add Triton MoE kernel configs for DBRX + A100
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds the optimal Triton kernel configs for DBRX on A100 GPUs.
TODO: Save the optimal configs for H100 GPUs.

vllm.title: [Core] [Bugfix] Refactor block manager subsystem for better testability
vllm.author: cadedaniel
vllm.bodyExcerpt:

The interfaces are such that the BlockAllocator
